---
title: "Beetle Classification Performance Analysis (Cicindela + Platydracus)"
output:
  html_notebook:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
---

# Deep Learning Model Performance for Beetle Taxonomy

This notebook analyzes classification performance of a multi-label deep learning model for identifying tiger beetle (*Cicindela*) and rove beetle (*Platydracus*) specimens. A single model is trained on the union of both genera; performance is examined per-genus across the three-level taxonomic hierarchy (genus / species / subspecies).

**Author**: B. de Medeiros, 2025

## Setup and Data Processing

```{r setup-and-load}
set.seed(1324)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggthemes)
library(cowplot)
```

The exported CSV (`test_results_<arch>.csv`) carries a `genus` column and a `taxonomic_level` column computed by the notebook. We recompute `taxonomic_level` here defensively (in case the CSV is older), using the label-format rule: capitalized → genus, `<genus>group_<group>` → species_group, exactly one `_` → species, two or more `_` → subspecies.

```{r data-processing}
performance_data <- read_csv("test_results_eva02_large_patch14_448.mim_m38m_ft_in22k_in1k.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    across(starts_with('micro'), ~ .x / 100),
    taxonomic_level = case_when(
      str_detect(Term, "^[A-Z]") ~ "genus",
      str_detect(Term, "^[a-z]+group_") ~ "species_group",
      str_count(Term, "_") == 1 ~ "species",
      TRUE ~ "subspecies"
    ),
    taxonomic_level = factor(taxonomic_level, levels = c("genus", "species_group", "species", "subspecies")),
    f1 = true_positives / (true_positives + (false_positives + false_negatives) / 2)
  ) %>%
  rename(
    taxon = Term,
    precision = micro_precision,
    recall = micro_recall,
    training_specimens = N_train,
    test_specimens = N
  )

glimpse(performance_data)
```

We also pull in the Platydracus species-group mapping (`images/Platydracus/species_groups.csv`) so each species/subspecies row can be tagged with its group. Keys are built the same way as in the training notebook: `platydracus_<species>[_<subsp>]`.

```{r species-group-mapping}
species_groups_raw <- read_csv("./species_groups.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

species_group_map <- species_groups_raw %>%
  mutate(
    parts = str_split(str_trim(full_taxonomy), "\\s+"),
    genus_part = map_chr(parts, 1),
    rest = map_chr(parts, ~ paste(.x[-1], collapse = "_"))
  ) %>%
  filter(genus_part == "Platydracus", rest != "") %>%
  transmute(
    taxon = paste0("platydracus_", rest),
    species_group = str_trim(species_group)
  )

# Propagate species-level group to subspecies rows that aren't listed explicitly.
species_level_map <- species_group_map %>%
  filter(str_count(taxon, "_") == 1) %>%
  rename(species_taxon = taxon, species_group_from_species = species_group)

performance_data <- performance_data %>%
  mutate(
    species_taxon = if_else(
      genus == "Platydracus" & taxonomic_level %in% c("species", "subspecies"),
      str_extract(taxon, "^platydracus_[^_]+"),
      NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  left_join(species_group_map, by = "taxon") %>%
  left_join(species_level_map, by = "species_taxon") %>%
  mutate(
    species_group = coalesce(species_group, species_group_from_species)
  ) %>%
  select(-species_taxon, -species_group_from_species)
```

## Per-Genus, Per-Level Summary Statistics

```{r helper-function}
calculate_averages <- function(data, ...) {
  data %>%
    group_by(...) %>%
    summarise(
      precision_equal = mean(precision, na.rm = TRUE),
      recall_equal = mean(recall, na.rm = TRUE),
      precision_weighted = weighted.mean(precision, training_specimens, na.rm = TRUE),
      recall_weighted = weighted.mean(recall, training_specimens, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_terms = n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
}
```

```{r summary-stats}
summary_stats <- calculate_averages(performance_data, genus, taxonomic_level)

plot_averages <- summary_stats %>%
  select(-n_terms) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(-genus, -taxonomic_level), names_to = 'metric') %>%
  mutate(
    weighted = str_detect(metric, '_weighted'),
    metric_clean = str_replace(metric, '^(.+?)_(equal|weighted)$', '\\1')
  ) %>%
  select(-metric) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = metric_clean, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(
    weighting_type = if_else(weighted, "Weighted by specimens", "Equal taxa")
  )

print("Summary Statistics by Genus and Taxonomic Level:")
summary_stats
```

## Precision-Recall Visualization (faceted by genus)

Genus-level classification is perfect for both *Cicindela* and *Platydracus* (precision = recall = 100%), so we omit it from the figures below and note it in text.

```{r prepare-plot-data}
plot_data <- performance_data %>%
  filter(taxonomic_level != "genus") %>%
  mutate(taxonomic_level = droplevels(taxonomic_level)) %>%
  arrange(desc(training_specimens))

plot_averages_sub <- plot_averages %>%
  filter(taxonomic_level != "genus") %>%
  mutate(taxonomic_level = droplevels(taxonomic_level))
```

```{r precision-recall-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
precision_recall_plot <- ggplot() +
  geom_jitter(
    data = plot_data,
    aes(y = precision, x = recall, color = training_specimens),
    width = 0.02,
    height = 0.02,
    alpha = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = plot_averages_sub,
    aes(y = precision, x = recall, shape = weighted),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(3, 4),
    name = 'Averaging',
    labels = c("taxa", 'specimens'),
    guide = 'none'
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    trans = 'log10',
    name = "Specimens\nin training",
    breaks = c(1, 10, 100, max(plot_data$training_specimens))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent, breaks = seq(0,1,by=.25), limits=c(-0.03,1.03)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, breaks = seq(0,1,by=.25), limits=c(-0.03,1.03)) +
  ggtitle("Precision and Recall") +
  xlab("Recall") +
  ylab("Precision") +
  facet_grid(genus ~ taxonomic_level) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text = element_text(size=9))

print(precision_recall_plot)
```

## Performance vs Training Data Size

```{r prepare-training-size-data}
training_size_data <- performance_data %>%
  arrange(desc(training_specimens)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("precision", "recall"),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )
```

```{r performance-vs-training-size, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
training_size_plot <- ggplot(training_size_data) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(x = training_specimens, y = value, color = genus),
    width = 0.03,
    height = 0.03,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  xlab("Training specimens (log scale)") +
  ylab("Performance") +
  facet_grid(taxonomic_level ~ metric) +
  theme_minimal()

print(training_size_plot)
```

## Key Findings Summary

```{r findings-summary}
cat("Dataset Overview:\n")
cat(sprintf("- Total taxa analyzed: %d\n", nrow(performance_data)))

for (g in sort(unique(performance_data$genus))) {
  gdata <- performance_data %>% filter(genus == g)
  cat(sprintf("\n%s (n_terms = %d):\n", g, nrow(gdata)))
  for (lvl in c("genus", "species", "subspecies")) {
    sub <- gdata %>% filter(taxonomic_level == lvl)
    if (nrow(sub) == 0) next
    row <- summary_stats %>% filter(genus == g, taxonomic_level == lvl)
    cat(sprintf("  %-10s  n=%3d  precision (equal)=%.1f%%  recall (equal)=%.1f%%  precision (wt)=%.1f%%  recall (wt)=%.1f%%\n",
                lvl, nrow(sub),
                100 * row$precision_equal, 100 * row$recall_equal,
                100 * row$precision_weighted, 100 * row$recall_weighted))
  }
}
```

## Training Sample Distribution

```{r distribution-plot, fig.width=6, fig.height=4}
dist_plot <- ggplot(plot_data) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(x = training_specimens, fill = genus),
    bins = 50,
    position = "stack"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_x_log10(breaks=c(1,2,5,10,20,50,100,200,500)) +
  ggtitle("Label frequency") +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab("Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text = element_text(size=9), legend.position = "bottom")

dist_plot
```

## F1 Score vs Training Sample Size

To spot whether any Platydracus species group is disproportionately hard, the
points/curves are split into one panel per group. Cicindela (no species groups
defined) appears as its own panel. The dashed gray curve in every panel is the
global Platydracus species-group-level smooth, repeated so each panel can be
read against the same baseline.

```{r f1-plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
f1_data <- plot_data %>%
  filter(taxonomic_level %in% c("species", "subspecies")) %>%
  mutate(
    panel = case_when(
      genus == "Cicindela" ~ "Cicindela",
      !is.na(species_group) ~ paste0("Platydracus: ", species_group),
      TRUE ~ "Platydracus: (ungrouped)"
    )
  )

# Baseline: Platydracus species/subspecies points, repeated in every panel.
baseline_data <- f1_data %>%
  filter(genus == "Platydracus") %>%
  select(training_specimens, f1)

f1_plot <- ggplot(f1_data, aes(x = training_specimens, y = f1)) +
  geom_smooth(
    data = baseline_data,
    aes(x = training_specimens, y = f1),
    method = "loess",
    span = 0.8,
    se = FALSE,
    color = gray(0.7),
    linetype = "dashed",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(color = genus),
    method = "loess",
    span = 0.8,
    se = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = genus, shape = taxonomic_level),
    width = 0.03,
    height = 0.03,
    alpha = 0.7,
    size = 1.6
  ) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent,
    breaks = seq(0, 1.00, by = 0.20)
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Genus") +
  scale_shape_discrete(name = "Level") +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 4) +
  ggtitle("F1 score by label frequency, split by Platydracus species group") +
  xlab("Number of training samples") +
  ylab("F1 score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 9), legend.position = "bottom")

f1_plot
```

```{r f1-by-group-summary}
f1_data %>%
  filter(genus == "Platydracus", !is.na(species_group)) %>%
  group_by(species_group) %>%
  summarise(
    n_taxa = n(),
    median_f1 = median(f1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_f1 = mean(f1, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_train = median(training_specimens),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(median_f1)
```

## Combined Performance Visualization

```{r extract-legend}
legend_AB <- get_legend(
  f1_plot + theme(
    legend.position = 'bottom',
    legend.box = 'vertical',
    legend.box.spacing = unit(0, 'pt'),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(-4, 'pt'),
    legend.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)
  )
)
legend_C  <- get_legend(
  precision_recall_plot +
    theme(legend.position = 'bottom', legend.box = 'horizontal') +
    guides(color = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 8, barheight = 0.6))
)
```

```{r combine-plots, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}
p_AB <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  dist_plot + theme(legend.position = 'none'),
  f1_plot + theme(legend.position = 'none'),
  ncol = 1,
  labels = "AUTO",
  align = 'hv',
  axis = 'lrtb'
)

top_row <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  p_AB,
  precision_recall_plot + theme(legend.position = 'none'),
  rel_widths = c(0.8, 1.2),
  nrow = 1,
  labels = c("", "C")
)

legend_row <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  legend_AB,
  legend_C,
  rel_widths = c(0.8, 1.2),
  nrow = 1
)

p <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  top_row,
  legend_row,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.14)
)

p
```

## Export Publication Figures

```{r save-figures}
ggsave(
  filename = 'taxonomy_metrics.pdf',
  plot = p,
  device = 'pdf',
  width = 11,
  height = 5,
  units = 'in',
  useDingbats = FALSE
)

ggsave(
  filename = 'taxonomy_metrics.png',
  plot = p,
  device = 'png',
  width = 11,
  height = 5,
  units = 'in',
  dpi = 300
)
```

## Individual Taxon Inspection

```{r taxon-lookup}
performance_data %>%
  filter(taxon %in% c("cicindela_sylvicola", "Cicindela", "Platydracus"))
```
